analysisSummary: Confirmed by tracing spawnTeammate() and TeammateRpc.start(). The child process is created before the ready handshake, and handshake failure only unwinds bookkeeping. Without an explicit termination step, a failed spawn attempt can leave a live orphan teammate process behind.
suggestedFix: On RPC ready-handshake failure, immediately terminate the spawned child with the same forced-termination path used by stop(), then clear the stored process reference before rethrowing.

analysisSummary: Confirmed by reading stop() and send(). stop() awaited a full RPC abort request before signalling the child process. If the child is alive but non-responsive, teardown can hang on the 60-second RPC timeout per worker before SIGTERM/SIGKILL is attempted.
suggestedFix: Make abort cooperative but bounded—race it against a short timeout, then start process termination immediately regardless of RPC responsiveness.

analysisSummary: Confirmed by tracing the new cleanup suppression logic. After `/team cleanup`, refresh refused to recreate config.json even after fresh task/worker state reappeared. That left the session in a partially rebuilt team state with missing config-driven behavior.
suggestedFix: Allow config bootstrap to resume automatically once new tasks or teammates exist, and clear the suppression flag when config has been re-established.
